growth_records.strain: SP3 background / engineered derivatives (e.g., SP526, SP1182)
growth_records.medium: Modified SOT (Spirulina-Ogawa-Terui) medium, partial NaHCO3->Na2CO3 replacement, photoautotrophic airlift photobioreactor culture
growth_records.cite: Jester et al. Development of spirulina for the manufacture and oral delivery of protein therapeutics. Nature Biotechnology (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41587-022-01249-7. [PMC9200632]
growth_records.snippet: At low densities spirulina can undergo exponential growth with a doubling time of 2–3 h. At densities greater than ~0.5 g l–1, growth becomes light limited...
growth_records.notes: Photoautotrophic (light-driven, no carbon substrate) growth of engineered spirulina platform strains in airlift photobioreactors; medium modified by partial replacement of NaHCO3 with Na2CO3, initial/controlled pH 9.8-10, 35°C, light 500-2500 umol/m2/s (Methods). Doubling time explicitly reported as a range (2-3 h) for the low-density exponential phase only (before growth becomes light-limited/linear at >~0.5 g/L biomass); growth_rate_per_h calculated as mu=ln2/T_d using midpoint T_d=2.5h => mu=0.693/2.5=0.277 h-1.

up.method: estimated from biomass carbon composition (ref 43) plus the maximum specific growth rate, assuming no metabolite excretion in log phase

growth_records.cite: Katsunori Yoshikawa et al. Construction of a Genome-Scale Metabolic Model of Arthrospira platensis NIES-39 and Metabolic Design for Cyanobacterial Bioproduction. PLoS ONE (2015). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0144430. [PMC4671677]
growth_records.method: reported (experimental; cited from ref 6). doubling_time calculated: td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.074=9.37 h
growth_records.snippet: the maximum specific growth rate of A. platensis NIES-39 was calculated to be 0.074 (/h) from culture results under various light intensity conditions [6]. The maximum CO2 uptake rate was estimated to be 3.1 mmol/gDW/h from the carbon compo
growth_records.notes: Autotrophic (photosynthetic) condition. EXPERIMENTAL specific growth rate mu=0.074/h is cited from ref 6 = Aikawa S, Izumi Y, Matsuda F et al., 'Synergistic enhancement of glycogen production in Arthrospira platensis by optimization of light intensity and nitrate supply', Bioresour Technol 108 (2012) 211-215, doi:10.1016/j.biortech.2012.01.004 (culture in SOT medium under various light intensities). Simulated/model mu=0.069/h is a PREDICTION (excluded as a rate). Doubling time calculated: td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.074=9.37 h. CO2 uptake 3.1 mmol/gDW/h is NOT directly measured: it was estimated from biomass carbon composition (ref 43) and mu, so is_experimental=false. Photon uptake left free. No secreted products reported (model assumes no excretion in log phase). pH not stated for autotrophic condition.

growth_records.medium: heterotrophic glucose medium (not specified in this paper; cited experiment)

up.method: calculated from experimental result of cited heterotrophic culture (ref 37)

growth_records.cite: Katsunori Yoshikawa et al. Construction of a Genome-Scale Metabolic Model of Arthrospira platensis NIES-39 and Metabolic Design for Cyanobacterial Bioproduction. PLoS ONE (2015). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0144430. [PMC4671677]
growth_records.method: reported (experimental; cited from ref 37). doubling_time calculated: td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.10=6.93 h
growth_records.snippet: The specific growth rate and specific glucose uptake rate were calculated from a previous experiment under heterotrophic conditions generated with glucose and without light illumination [37]... glucose uptake, 0.16 mmol/gDW/h calculated fro
growth_records.notes: Heterotrophic (dark, glucose) condition. EXPERIMENTAL specific growth rate mu=0.10/h and specific glucose uptake 0.16 mmol/gDW/h are both cited/derived from ref 37 (a previous heterotrophic culture experiment of A. platensis NIES-39; ref 37 is the reference the authors cite for the experimental glucose growth data). Simulated/model mu=0.11/h is a PREDICTION (excluded). Doubling time calculated: td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.10=6.93 h. Medium composition for this heterotrophic condition is not given in this paper (defined in cited ref 37), so medium_composition_stated=false. Temperature and pH not stated for this condition. No secreted products reported.
